searchTerms: Sono Bisque Doll wa Koi wo Suru

synopsis: Tokyo teens Kei and Masaru are killed by a subway train but awaken in a room with an ominous black orb that gives them weapons, suits and orders. Fighting bizarre alien monstrosities in a deadly game, will they win their freedom or die for the final time? (Source: Dark Horse)

tags.name: Survival
tags.description: Centers around the struggle to live in spite of extreme obstacles.

tags.name: Seinen
tags.description: Target demographic is adult males.

tags.name: Gore
tags.description: Prominently features graphic bloodshed and violence.

tags.name: Aliens
tags.description: Prominently features extraterrestrial lifeforms.

tags.name: Tragedy
tags.description: Centers around tragic events and unhappy endings.

tags.name: Death Game
tags.description: Features characters participating in a game, where failure results in death.

tags.name: Urban
tags.description: Partly or completely set in a city.

tags.name: Guns
tags.description: Prominently features the use of guns in combat.

tags.name: Philosophy
tags.description: Relating or devoted to the study of the fundamental nature of knowledge, reality, and existence.

tags.name: Cosmic Horror
tags.description: A type of horror that emphasizes human insignificance in the grand scope of cosmic reality; fearing the unknown and being powerless to fight it.

tags.name: Battle Royale
tags.description: Centers around a fierce group competition, often violent and with only one winner.

tags.name: Nudity
tags.description: Features a character wearing no clothing or exposing intimate body parts.

tags.name: Heterosexual
tags.description: Prominently features a romance between a man and a woman, not inherently sexual.

tags.name: Ensemble Cast
tags.description: Features a large cast of characters with (almost) equal screen time and importance to the plot.

tags.name: Rape
tags.description: Features non-consensual sexual penetration.

tags.name: Super Power
tags.description: Prominently features characters with special abilities that allow them to do what would normally be physically or logically impossible.

tags.name: Terrorism
tags.description: Centers around the activities of a terrorist or terrorist organization.

tags.name: Vampire
tags.description: Prominently features a character who is a vampire.

tags.name: Assassins
tags.description: Centers around characters who murder people as a profession.

tags.name: Clone
tags.description: Prominently features a character who is an artificial exact copy of another organism.

tags.name: Female Harem
tags.description: Main cast features the protagonist plus several female characters who are romantically interested in them.

tags.name: Youkai
tags.description: Prominently features supernatural creatures from Japanese folklore.

tags.name: CGI
tags.description: Prominently features scenes created with computer-generated imagery.

tags.name: Dinosaurs
tags.description: Prominently features Dinosaurs, prehistoric reptiles that went extinct millions of years ago.

tags.name: Age Gap
tags.description: Prominently features romantic relations between people with a significant age difference.

tags.name: Anti-Hero
tags.description: Features a protagonist who lacks conventional heroic attributes and may be considered a borderline villain.

tags.name: Crime
tags.description: Centers around unlawful activities punishable by the state or other authority.

tags.name: Trains
tags.description: Prominently features trains.

title: Shino & Ren
synopsis: The tomboy and the quiet girl don't seem to have much in common at first glance, except that they're head over heels in love. Ren is loud, brash, and possessive—a far cry from Shino's calmer, more reserved demeanor. But hey, sparks fly in the most unlikely cases, and this couple can barely keep their hands off each other. Between Ren's aggressive flirting and Shino's adorable personality, if one of them isn't blushing, whoever's reading along with them sure will be. (Source: Yen Press)

synopsis: “These three could destroy the country all by themselves if they wanted… But there was one thing they just couldn't get.” For the three strongest adventurers in the world, finding a man strong enough to be with them is a herculean task. He simply doesn’t exist. With that in mind, they decide to take on a perfect novice—the level-zero, unremarkable Cross Alacart—and train him up to be their perfect match. Freshly expelled from Adventurers’ school, Cross sets his eyes on redemption; training hard and learning to believe in himself along the way. (Source: Comikey)

title: Lazy Girl Momogusa
titleNative: 桃草さんがものぐさすぎる!!
synopsis: Souji Hataki is an ordinary high school boy who has just landed the perfect new part-time job as a housekeeper for the house of Momogusa. What’s so significant about that? It just so happens to be the home of the most beautiful girl in school! Perfect, pretty, and popular, Momogusa-san is amazing at everything she does and now Hataki will have a chance to see her in her element. But, he quickly learns something shocking: Momogusa is incredibly lazy at home! The moment she’s home from school, she seems to transform into a totally different version of herself and even expects to be pampered! Somehow she grows fond of Hataki and even begins to let him pamper her. Could this be the start of some sort of relationship or is Hataki just a glorified manservant? (Source: Seven Seas Entertainment)

title: Chie Aru Koibito
synopsis: The protagonists of "Chie Aru Koibito," Ushidome-kun and Shirakaba-san, are both intelligent class representatives who judge things rationally. Even though their classmates extol the two as a perfect match, Shirakaba-san declares that she "doesn't have even a second to lose herself in love." Ushidome-kun is honestly shocked by this, but he also appeals that he is "not in a state to fall in love," which upsets Shirakaba-san. They are both too conscious of each other, and the rationality of the two explosive people is on the verge of collapse. This is the love story of a male and female honor student who cannot be honest with each other and are tormented by their own worldly desires. (Source: Natalie, translated)

synopsis: In the spring of his first year of high school, Michiko Takase decided that "school life will not work hard." However, the seat next to it was a female student full of “suspicious rumors” and takugi (Hakanagi) Kasumi! Michiru tries to keep a distance while wearing a bandage on the eye band, but the two are in a relationship of "compensating" from chance ...!? It's hilarious, but I'm going to do my best! Zako Kawa ♡ Youth watching love comedy with heroine! ! ! ! (Source: Hero's, translated)

title: Ittsuuu
synopsis: Sagami was dragged into sneaking into the high school that he and his two other friends were attempting to be accepted into after they graduate from middle school. While sneaking around, observing the high school girls, Sagami noticed one girl that stood out. It was love at first sight. He took a picture of her with his cell, and now uses it as his "reward" for studying harder to get into the high school. Due to this "reward" system, he successfully got accepted into the high school where the girl of his dreams attends. Now, an odd and awkward high school love life begins for Sagami. (Source: MangaHelpers)

title: Millenium Mirage
synopsis: "Let’s make a child together!!" is what a girl who appeared out of the blue said. A pretty onee-sama and a cute girl, middle school student Takao Reiichi’s destiny is going to change in a big way since he met these two girls! Was their encounter a coincidence? Or something is bound to happen? Falling in love with the opposite sex and friendship?! Here comes another new love comedy!

title: Transformers Legends
titleNative: トランスフォーマーレジェンズ
synopsis: Starting off as 4koma and published online to promote the titular Transformers Legends toyline, with special Bonus Edition (出張版 Shutchōban) chapters of the comic packed with the toys themselves, it Initially showcased the misadventures of the Beast Wars cast re-imagined as adult Transformers fans, before eventually taking a more story-driven approach and attempting to connect all the entries of the Japanese Generation 1 anime and manga continuity into one storyline. Heavily marketed towards adult collectors and otaku in the face of the brand's dwindling popularity with children after TakaraTomy's largely unsuccessful first attempt in the mid-2000s, it attempted to change the image of the Transformers franchise, with much more "adult", often ecchi-filled humour and numerous references to fan culture. Ulltimately concluded when TakaraTomy began a brand unification with Hasbro starting with Power of the Primes, deciding to mainly sell Hasbro toys as is with minimal changes to packaging. It was eventually succeeded by the more action-oriented Generations Selects Special Comic promoting Hasbro and TakaraTomy's joint Generations Selects imprint. (Source: TFwiki, edited)
